authority_boundaries: Packets may prepare or recommend the next review/decision surface, but do not themselves execute it.
Human acceptance nodes authorize only the bounded future unit named by the receipt/readout.
Bounded execution nodes consume prior authority and do not authorize a second execution.
Commits preserve artifacts and receipts; commit existence is not semantic authority.
Closure/readiness nodes expose a next decision surface without running it.
Meta-handoff nodes are outside the C8 runtime path and do not alter C8 authority.
commit_verification_note: 41f876f7ab4a971f571a0ee45ad2ca7d18996598 was not a local Git commit; the source-visible commit for the same acceptance node is 41f876f7a18581304fc1e4538d823519c6789f84.

edges.not_authorized: The packet itself does not perform the acceptance or execute downstream work.
edges.why_lawful: The prior packet/readout stops at a review boundary and requires an explicit human acceptance node.
